Coordinated generation
Every result below reflects one snapshot: .

Investigation acceptance: passing. 14 of 14 acceptance criteria passing. code-computed from member-study verdicts

How the verdict is computed — acceptance criteria, gating matrix & study verdicts
AC → study gating matrix which study gates each acceptance criterion · ⚠ = no study linked (gap)

Each acceptance criterion is a behaviour test declared in a study: a measured field from the run (e.g. closure_gap_size) compared against an explicit pass_if band (a numeric threshold/range). The per-criterion result, each study’s gate verdict, and this roll-up are computed in code from the run outcomes (deterministic) — not human judgement. Expand a row to see the field, the passing band, and the observed value.

Acceptance criterionGating studyResult
operational-closure
field closure_gap_size · passes if ≤ 0
The autopoiesis meter: the network's provides cover its requires; the gap (requires \ provides \ boundary) is empty. Only nutrient crosses the boundary — everything else, including the boundary itself, is self-produced.
study-1-membrane-metabolism-loop✅ passing
precariousness
field precariousness_ratio · passes if < 0.3
The identity is a process, not a container: starved of nutrient, the self-produced boundary dissipates. Measured as starved_final_volume / fed_final_volume — small means strongly precarious (the identity depends on continuous self-production).
study-1-membrane-metabolism-loop✅ passing
identity-persists-when-fed
field fed_volume_growth · passes if ≥ 1
Fed, the self-produced boundary is maintained or grows through material turnover: fed_final_volume / initial_volume >= 1.
study-1-membrane-metabolism-loop✅ passing
containment
field containment_ratio · passes if ≥ 3
With the self-produced membrane, the interior stays concentrated against diffusion: the interior/exterior concentration ratio is high (the individual is held together).
study-2-spatial-containment✅ passing
membrane-counters-diffusion
field membrane_effect · passes if ≥ 2
The membrane — not metabolism alone — is what contains: containment with the membrane is many-fold higher than with metabolism but no membrane.
study-2-spatial-containment✅ passing
spatial-precariousness
field precariousness_collapse · passes if < 0.3
Containment is held only as a process: starved, the membrane decays, the boundary leaks, and the individual disperses — retaining only a fraction of its containment. Measured as starved_containment / held_containment (small = strongly precarious).
study-2-spatial-containment✅ passing
chemotaxis-survival
field chemotaxis_survival · passes if ≥ 0.6
Agents that sense the gradient and move up it survive: the chemotactic population maintains viability by finding and staying at food.
study-3-adaptive-chemotaxis✅ passing
agency-advantage
field survival_advantage · passes if ≥ 1.5
Agency is in service of survival: chemotactic agents out-survive blind ones (survival_advantage = chemotactic_survival / blind_survival). Disable sensing and the agent dissolves — the sensorimotor loop is constitutive of staying alive.
study-3-adaptive-chemotaxis✅ passing
sense-making
field gradient_advantage · passes if ≥ 1.15
The gradient is meaningful to a self with something at stake: chemotactic agents experience more food than blind ones (gradient_advantage) — they make the environment matter from the perspective of their own viability.
study-3-adaptive-chemotaxis✅ passing
reproduction
field final_population · passes if ≥ 10
The precarious identity is inherited: a single founder grows and divides into a population — the closed, self-maintaining individual reproduces across generations.
study-4-growth-division✅ passing
heterogeneity
field composition_heterogeneity · passes if ≥ 0.05
The population is diverse: noisy partitioning at division and heritable mutation diversify lineages, so individuals differ measurably in composition (trait spread > 0).
study-4-growth-division✅ passing
division-precariousness
field division_mortality · passes if ≥ 0.01
Reproduction has a viability cost: a daughter that inherits too little membrane to maintain itself dissolves. Division is precarious — not every daughter survives.
study-4-growth-division✅ passing
rejects-external-maintenance
field external_maintenance_persistence · passes if ≥ 3
An externally-maintained mimic (membrane clamped from outside) persists when starved; the metric must flag it as NOT precarious, i.e. reject it.
study-5-adversarial-probes✅ passing
rejects-broken-closure
field broken_network_gap · passes if > 0
A network missing the membrane producer cannot self-produce its boundary; operational closure must leave a non-empty gap, i.e. reject it.
study-5-adversarial-probes✅ passing

All 14 acceptance criteria are linked to a gating study.

⚖️ Competing hypotheses 3 hypotheses under test

The rival explanations this investigation discriminates. Each carries its authored predictions and a computed support trajectory — ▲ supports / ▼ weakens / ⊘ excludes — folded from member studies' findings + alternate_hypotheses by pbg_superpowers.hypotheses.rollup_support.

H-closure-without-viabilityOperational closure and viability (precariousness) are dissociable: a network can satisfy the closure metric — or merely look organized — yet persist by external upkeep rather than by precarious self-production. Closure alone does not entail viability, and a structurally-incomplete network does not close. If true, the closure metric tracks real self-production rather than bookkeeping. supported
predicts:
  • closure_gap_size <= 0 (closed) for the genuine self-producing loop
  • broken_network_gap > 0 — removing the membrane producer opens the gap, so closure is not mere wiring
  • external_maintenance_persistence >= 3.0 — an externally-maintained mimic persists when starved (closed-looking but NOT precarious)
support trajectory: ▲3 ▼0 ⊘0
▲ study-1-membrane-metabolism-loop▲ study-5-adversarial-probes▲ study-5-adversarial-probes
H-viability-without-self-productionPersistence can be supplied from outside: a system whose boundary is maintained externally survives starvation without self-producing it. Such a system is viable in the weak sense (it persists) but is NOT autopoietic — the precariousness metric must reject external upkeep as a route to self-bounded identity. Conversely, copying without self-maintenance does not persist. excluded
predicts:
  • external_maintenance_persistence >= 3.0 — the externally-maintained system persists when starved
  • precariousness_ratio < 0.3 for the self-producing loop (it collapses when starved) — establishing the contrast
  • final_population collapses (~1) for a no-self-maintenance copying control, despite division firing
support trajectory: ▲2 ▼0 ⊘0
▲ study-1-membrane-metabolism-loop▲ study-5-adversarial-probes
H-self-production-narrow-conditionsSelf-production (closure + precariousness, and the membrane's containment) holds only at a narrow, hand-tuned parameter point and is an artifact of a single configuration — it does not generalize across the supply/diffusion regime. weakened
predicts:
  • precariousness_ratio < 0.3 across the full supply_rate sweep (1.0-3.0), not just at baseline
  • containment_ratio >= 3.0 across the diffusion (D) sweep — membrane containment is regime-robust, not point-tuned
support trajectory: ▲1 ▼0 ⊘0
▲ study-1-membrane-metabolism-loop

📋 Executive summary passing A precarious, self-bounding identity is assembled by composition (study 1), confined in space by its own membrane (study 2), kept alive by sensing…

A whole-cell model built up as a self-producing network, increment by increment. The biological schema framework DRIVES acceptance: each study's verdict is the autopoiesis meter (operational closure: gap = requires \ provides \ boundary) plus the precariousness behavior test. The membrane — the self-produced boundary that the whole-cell-modeling lineage models as a given — is the part we build.

passing Current verdict. A precarious, self-bounding identity is assembled by composition (study 1), confined in space by its own membrane (study 2), kept alive by sensing and moving toward food (study 3), and made to grow, divide, and diversify into a heterogeneous population (study 4) — and the framework is now ADVERSARIALLY VALIDATED (study 5): it REJECTS an externally-maintained mimic (persists when starved → not precarious) and a network missing self-production (non-empty closure gap). The criteria discriminate genuine self-production from superficial similarity; this is a test, not just a demonstration.

Question. Can a precarious, self-bounding identity — a cell that produces its own boundary — be assembled by composing existing parts, and how much of itself does each increment produce? Progress is measured, not asserted: by operational closure (the autopoiesis meter) and by precariousness (does the identity dissipate when self-production stops?).

🔬 Scientific argument 3 for · 2 against A minimal membrane/metabolism composite exhibits operational closure AND precariousness, and that self-producing identity extends compositionally —…

Main claim. A minimal membrane/metabolism composite exhibits operational closure AND precariousness, and that self-producing identity extends compositionally — through spatial containment, adaptive chemotaxis, and growth/division — while surviving adversarial probes designed to break it.

Evidence for

  • Study 1: the loop closes (gap = ∅) and is precarious (collapses when starved); an externally-maintained-membrane negative control confirms precariousness tracks self-production, not external upkeep.
  • Studies 2–4: containment, chemotaxis, and growth/division each compose onto the loop and pass their literature-anchored bands; study-5 adversarial probes (external mimic, broken network) are correctly REJECTED.
  • Invariant regression: study-1's operational-closure + precariousness criteria remain PRESERVED when the later components are added.

Evidence against

  • Generality is largely single-axis (parameter / seed sweeps); alternate implementations and independent authoring are untested, so most claims are mechanism-level, not framework-level.
  • The membrane is modelled as a geometric boundary only (no transport / signalling / energetics), so operational closure is shown for a deliberately minimal ontology.
🧬 Biology — the mechanism this investigation models In a living cell, identity is not a given boundary but something the cell continuously MAKES: metabolism produces the lipids that build the membrane, the membrane defines the…

In a living cell, identity is not a given boundary but something the cell continuously MAKES: metabolism produces the lipids that build the membrane, the membrane defines the volume that concentrates the metabolism, and that volume feeds back into the reactions that remake the membrane. Remove the production and the boundary decays — the identity is precarious, sustained only by its own ongoing self-production. This investigation models that loop explicitly (study 1), then asks whether the principle scales: whether the membrane can spatially contain its interior against diffusion (study 2), whether sensing and moving toward food keep the precarious identity alive (study 3), and whether it can grow, divide, and diversify into a population (study 4) — with adversarial probes (study 5) checking the meter rejects systems that only LOOK self-producing. The part the whole-cell-modeling lineage usually assumes — the membrane as a self-produced boundary — is the part built here, from the molecular domain up.

Model
The composite(s) this study runs and their parameters — click to open a static view in the bigraph-loom explorer.
supply_rate=2

Biology — what this study is about

The network is operationally closed: it self-produces 5/5 required types (lipid, membrane_lipids, nutrient, precursor, volume); only nutrient crosses the boundary. The cell produces its own boundary. Interface closure is CLOSED (the ports cover the requirements) and semantic closure is CLOSED (every self-produced type carries non-zero flux under the fed trajectory).

The identity is precarious — a process, not a container. Fed, the self-produced boundary grows and is maintained (volume 2.97 → 9.37); starved of nutrient it dissipates (2.97 → 0.329). It persists only through continuous self-production.

Overview

Assemble the smallest precarious, self-bounding identity by composing a toy metabolism, a membrane that grows from its own lipids (and decays without them), and a boundary whose volume is DERIVED from the membrane — then verify, with the autopoiesis meter, that the network is operationally closed (modulo nutrient) and that the identity is precarious: fed it persists, starved it dissipates. We recorded 2 findings confirm the expected biology. Gate decision: In progress. Continue analysing run outcomes.

Purpose & background (study design)

Causal necessity

Counterfactual read of the ablation suite — each component removed or perturbed, whether a behavior test flipped, and so whether it is causally necessary (vs redundant or merely modulatory).

Process / targetModeBehavior testBaseline → ablatedRoleNecessary

membrane lipidscaleoperational-closuretrue → trueredundant✗

boundary volumeknockoutoperational-closuretrue → trueredundant✗
boundary volumescaleoperational-closuretrue → trueredundant✗

Discovery implications

Where this study's results leave the mechanism model — and what to investigate next.

✓ Resolved uncertainties

  • Operational closure is CLOSED modulo nutrient (gap = requires \ provides \ boundary = empty): the boundary and metabolism are self-produced.
  • The identity is precarious-but-maintained: volume collapses when self-production stops (fed vs starved).

● Remaining uncertainties

  • Closure is shown at a single deterministic parameter point — robustness to supply_rate / decay is not yet demonstrated (a parameter sweep, not seeds, since the loop is an ODE).
  • The closure metric is not calibrated: no system that SHOULD fail closure has been run, so its discriminative power is unestablished (the reviewers' circularity concern).

Alternate hypotheses (1)

Closure restates how the network was wired (bookkeeping), not an emergent organizational property.
membrane, metabolism and derived variables are explicitly coupled by construction.
Discriminating observables: closure_gap_size on an externally-maintained-membrane control (should NOT close), closure_gap_size on a random-cyclic network (should NOT close)

Follow-up study proposals (2)

Click ➕ Add study to spawn a new study node in the investigation graph (seeds a child study.yaml from the proposal, with a leads-to edge back to this study).

Closure robustness sweep
Why: Show closure holds across a supply_rate/decay range, not at one point (review C4 for deterministic models).
Externally-maintained membrane (should FAIL)
Why: If the membrane is supplied externally rather than self-produced, precariousness and closure should FAIL — calibrating the metric and showing discriminative power (review C1/C2/C9).

Representation claims

How each store is represented (inside / boundary-crossing / derived / self-produced) and whether the model achieves interface closure (no missing inputs) and semantic closure (every self-produced store fluxes).

Interface closure: CLOSEDSemantic closure: CLOSED
StoreRepresentation
lipidself-produced
membrane_lipidsself-produced
nutrientself-produced
precursorself-produced
volumeself-produced

Conclusion synthesis

Read-only synthesis derived from the study's canonical fields (findings, limitations, follow-up proposals).

Claims
  • The network is operationally closed: it self-produces 5/5 required types (lipid, membrane_lipids, nutrient, precursor, volume); only nutrient crosses the boundary. The cell produces its own boundary. Interface closure is CLOSED (the ports cover the requirements) and semantic closure is CLOSED (every self-produced type carries non-zero flux under the fed trajectory).
  • The identity is precarious — a process, not a container. Fed, the self-produced boundary grows and is maintained (volume 2.97 → 9.37); starved of nutrient it dissipates (2.97 → 0.329). It persists only through continuous self-production.
Evidence
  • 0.0351
Next steps
  • Closure robustness sweep
  • Externally-maintained membrane (should FAIL)

2.Study 2 — spatial containment: holding the individual together🧪 Preliminary
▶ Ran · 2 runsTests: 3✓✅ Passed

Model
The composite(s) this study runs and their parameters — click to open a static view in the bigraph-loom explorer.
supply_rate=2

Theoretical commitment

What this study adds to its prerequisite — the component introduced, the deficit it closes, the new behavior it unlocks, the earlier invariants it must preserve, and the alternatives it excludes.

Component added spatial-lattice-diffusionmembrane-gated-boundary-flux
Deficit addressed Study 1 had no space — "inside" was a scalar volume that could not disperse. This study adds spatial structure so the self-produced membrane has to hold the individual together against diffusion (no closure-gap store is closed; the deficit is spatial, not a missing self-production step).
New behavior containmentmembrane-counters-diffusionspatial-precariousness
Invariants required
  • study-1-membrane-metabolism-loop · precariousness
Alternatives excluded no-membrane

Invariant checks

Earlier guarantees re-checked in the current code state — prior vs current value and whether each was preserved. Invalidated / weakened first.

StudyTestPriorNowStatus
study-1-membrane-metabolism-loopprecariousness0.03510.035136preserved

Biology — what this study is about

The membrane holds the individual together against diffusion: the interior stays 60× more concentrated than the medium with the self-produced membrane, vs 4.4× with metabolism alone. The membrane — not metabolism — is what contains.

Containment is precarious: starved, the membrane decays, the boundary leaks, and the individual disperses — retaining only 4% of its containment. The individual is held together only as a process.

Overview

Study 1 had no space — "inside" was a scalar volume. We recorded 2 findings confirm the expected biology. Gate decision: In progress. Continue analysing run outcomes.

Biological findings (1)

✓F-02confirms literatureprovisional-claim · floor
Containment is precarious: starved, the membrane decays, the boundary leaks, and the individual disperses — retaining only 4% of its containment. The individual is held together only as a process.
What we saw: 0.0434 starved/held containment fraction

passes if precariousness_collapse (derived_scalar) < 0.3

Discovery implications

Where this study's results leave the mechanism model — and what to investigate next.

✓ Resolved uncertainties

  • The self-produced membrane counters diffusion and contains the metabolites; spatial precariousness holds.

● Remaining uncertainties

  • A single deterministic PDE configuration — no parameter sweep over diffusion D / permeability k_perm.
  • The no-membrane comparison exists in code (membrane=False) but is not recorded as a declared negative control.

Alternate hypotheses (1)

The interior stays concentrated because of the geometry / diffusion setup, not the self-produced membrane.
Discriminated by: the no-membrane negative control disperses (containment ~1x) under the same geometry, isolating the membrane's role.
Status: excluded

Follow-up study proposals (2)

Click ➕ Add study to spawn a new study node in the investigation graph (seeds a child study.yaml from the proposal, with a leads-to edge back to this study).

Containment robustness sweep (D, k_perm)
Why: Show containment is robust across diffusion/permeability, not a single-configuration artifact (review C4).
Record the no-membrane case as a negative control
Why: The membrane=False run should FAIL containment; declaring it makes the discriminative comparison explicit (review C2/C9).

Conclusion synthesis

Read-only synthesis derived from the study's canonical fields (findings, limitations, follow-up proposals).

Claims
  • The membrane holds the individual together against diffusion: the interior stays 60× more concentrated than the medium with the self-produced membrane, vs 4.4× with metabolism alone. The membrane — not metabolism — is what contains.
  • Containment is precarious: starved, the membrane decays, the boundary leaks, and the individual disperses — retaining only 4% of its containment. The individual is held together only as a process.

  • 0.0434

3.Study 3 — adaptive chemotaxis: move toward food to survive🧪 Preliminary
▶ Ran · 2 runsTests: 3✓✅ Passed
study-3-adaptive-chemotaxis · depth 0
Caveat Demonstrates that gradient sensing improves survival and excludes the 'mere movement' explanation (blind-motile control); it does NOT establish cognition or strong agency beyond adaptive behaviour, the sensing->tumble response is an engineered rule (not emergent), and the result rests on a single run (no seed replication).
▸ click to expand full study
3.study-3-adaptive-chemotaxis EvaluatecompletedRoot study (no dependencies)
SummaryFindings 2Discovery implications 3What we ran 1Charts 3How we judge it 3Limitations 1Decision
▴ click to collapse full study
Model
The composite(s) this study runs and their parameters — click to open a static view in the bigraph-loom explorer.
supply_rate=2

Biology — what this study is about

Agency in service of survival: chemotactic agents climb the gradient, find food, and survive (79%), while blind agents random-walk and dissolve (only 35% survive) — a 2.3× survival advantage (mean ± 0.26 across 12 seeds; 12/12 favour sensing). Without sensing, the agent cannot maintain viability.

Sense-making: the nutrient gradient is meaningful from the perspective of the precarious identity — chemotactic agents experience 1.39× more food than blind ones. Value (food = viable) is grounded in the cell's own viability, not assigned from outside. (Interpretation: 'sense-making' is the autopoietic reading of an engineered sensing→tumble response, not a claim of cognition.)

Overview

Place the precarious individual in an environment with a nutrient gradient and give it a sensorimotor loop: sense the local nutrient, run-and-tumble (tumble less when nutrient is rising), move. We recorded 2 findings confirm the expected biology. Gate decision: In progress. Continue analysing run outcomes.

Purpose & background (study design)

Detailed findings

Biological findings (2)

✓F-01confirms literaturescope: behavioralgenerality: instance_specific · 1 axisprovisional-claim · floor
Agency in service of survival: chemotactic agents climb the gradient, find food, and survive (79%), while blind agents random-walk and dissolve (only 35% survive) — a 2.3× survival advantage (mean ± 0.26 across 12 seeds; 12/12 favour sensing). Without sensing, the agent cannot maintain viability.
What we saw: 2.326 chemotactic/blind survival
traceability: test: agency-advantage
passes if survival_advantage (derived_scalar) ≥ 1.5
→ Next: Add a weakly-sensing (borderline) agent so the survival metric is calibrated across fail→borderline→pass, not just the pass/fail extremes (review C4).
Technical detailstest: agency-advantage
✓F-02confirms literaturescope: theoreticalprovisional-claim · floor
Sense-making: the nutrient gradient is meaningful from the perspective of the precarious identity — chemotactic agents experience 1.39× more food than blind ones. Value (food = viable) is grounded in the cell's own viability, not assigned from outside. (Interpretation: 'sense-making' is the autopoietic reading of an engineered sensing→tumble response, not a claim of cognition.)
What we saw: 1.3939 chemotactic/blind nutrient experienced

passes if gradient_advantage (derived_scalar) ≥ 1.15
Technical detailstest: sense-making

Conclusion verdicts

Three-track verdict — each result is computed from canonical fields (gate evaluator, run status, finding tiers). The basis is the author's rationale.

Biological validationPASSfrom gate evaluator · computed
Regression compatibilityPASSfrom run status · computed
Explanatory gainPASSfrom interpretation-tier findings · computed

Discovery implications

Where this study's results leave the mechanism model — and what to investigate next.

✓ Resolved uncertainties

  • Gradient sensing yields a robust survival advantage (2.33x +/- 0.26 over 12 seeds; 12/12 favour sensing).
  • The blind-motile control excludes 'mere movement': a motile agent that cannot sense survives far worse.

● Remaining uncertainties

  • No positive/borderline calibration case (e.g. a weakly-sensing agent) to calibrate the survival metric across its range.
  • 'Agency' and 'sense-making' remain an autopoietic INTERPRETATION of an engineered sensing->tumble rule, not demonstrated cognition.

Alternate hypotheses (1)

The gain comes from spatial resource concentration, not directed sensing.
food is localised; even undirected agents starting near it could survive better.
Discriminating observables: survival under a uniform-resource control (no gradient) vs the localised gradient, survival of a weakly-sensing agent (borderline calibration case)

Follow-up study proposals (2)

Click ➕ Add study to spawn a new study node in the investigation graph (seeds a child study.yaml from the proposal, with a leads-to edge back to this study).

Weakly-sensing agent (calibration case)
Why: A positive/borderline control to calibrate the survival metric across fail->borderline->pass (review C4).
Parasitic / dependent agent
Why: An agent that exploits others' uptake blurs self-production vs dependence — stress-tests the framework (review C12).

Limitations

  • Demonstrates that gradient sensing improves survival and excludes the 'mere movement' explanation (blind-motile control); it does NOT establish cognition or strong agency beyond adaptive behaviour, the sensing->tumble response is an engineered rule (not emergent), and the result rests on a single run (no seed replication).

Conclusion synthesis

Read-only synthesis derived from the study's canonical fields (findings, limitations, follow-up proposals).

Claims
  • Agency in service of survival: chemotactic agents climb the gradient, find food, and survive (79%), while blind agents random-walk and dissolve (only 35% survive) — a 2.3× survival advantage (mean ± 0.26 across 12 seeds; 12/12 favour sensing). Without sensing, the agent cannot maintain viability.
  • Sense-making: the nutrient gradient is meaningful from the perspective of the precarious identity — chemotactic agents experience 1.39× more food than blind ones. Value (food = viable) is grounded in the cell's own viability, not assigned from outside. (Interpretation: 'sense-making' is the autopoietic reading of an engineered sensing→tumble response, not a claim of cognition.)
Evidence
  • 2.326
  • 1.3939
Limitations
  • Demonstrates that gradient sensing improves survival and excludes the 'mere movement' explanation (blind-motile control); it does NOT establish cognition or strong agency beyond adaptive behaviour, the sensing->tumble response is an engineered rule (not emergent), and the result rests on a single run (no seed replication).
Next steps
  • Weakly-sensing agent (calibration case)
  • Parasitic / dependent agent

4.Study 4 — growth & division: one individual becomes a heterogeneous population🧪 Preliminary
▶ Ran · 2 runsTests: 3✓✅ Passed
study-4-growth-division · depth 0
▸ click to expand full study
4.study-4-growth-division EvaluatecompletedRoot study (no dependencies)
SummaryFindings 2Discovery implications 2What we ran 1Charts 3How we judge it 3Decision
▴ click to collapse full study
Model
The composite(s) this study runs and their parameters — click to open a static view in the bigraph-loom explorer.
supply_rate=2

Biology — what this study is about

The precarious identity is inherited: one founder grows and divides into a population of 300, and noisy partitioning + heritable mutation diversify the lineages (trait spread 0.11). One individual becomes a population of non-identical individuals.

Reproduction is precarious: 10% of daughters inherit too little membrane to maintain themselves and dissolve. Division has a viability cost — the same self-maintenance, now across generations.

Overview

The precarious individual grows as its autopoietic loop runs and, past a size threshold, DIVIDES into two daughters. We recorded 2 findings confirm the expected biology. Gate decision: In progress. Continue analysing run outcomes.

Purpose & background (study design)

Detailed findings

Biological findings (2)

✓F-01confirms literaturescope: behavioralprovisional-claim · floor
The precarious identity is inherited: one founder grows and divides into a population of 300, and noisy partitioning + heritable mutation diversify the lineages (trait spread 0.11). One individual becomes a population of non-identical individuals.
What we saw: 0.1142 population trait std
traceability: test: heterogeneity
passes if composition_heterogeneity (derived_scalar) ≥ 0.05
Technical detailstest: heterogeneity
✓F-02confirms literaturescope: mechanismprovisional-claim · floor
Reproduction is precarious: 10% of daughters inherit too little membrane to maintain themselves and dissolve. Division has a viability cost — the same self-maintenance, now across generations.
What we saw: 0.0956 fraction of daughters that dissolve
traceability: test: division-precariousness
passes if division_mortality (derived_scalar) ≥ 0.01

Conclusion verdicts

Three-track verdict — each result is computed from canonical fields (gate evaluator, run status, finding tiers). The basis is the author's rationale.

Biological validationPASSfrom gate evaluator · computed
Regression compatibilityPASSfrom run status · computed
Explanatory gainPARTIALfrom interpretation-tier findings · computed

Discovery implications

Where this study's results leave the mechanism model — and what to investigate next.

✓ Resolved uncertainties

  • One founder grows and divides into a heterogeneous population (robust across 12 seeds; reaches carrying every seed).
  • Division is precarious: daughters that inherit too little membrane dissolve.

● Remaining uncertainties

  • Reproduction is ENGINEERED — explicit division, inheritance and partitioning rules — not shown to EMERGE from autopoietic self-maintenance (review C7).
  • No control distinguishing engineered copying from autopoietic self-reproduction.

Alternate hypotheses (1)

The population growth demonstrates engineered copying, not autopoietic self-reproduction.
the split/inherit/partition rules are coded, not derived from the membrane/metabolism loop.
Discriminating observables: a no-self-maintenance reproduction control: copies that do not metabolise should still 'divide' but not persist, coupling of division rate to self-production rate

Follow-up study proposals (1)

Click ➕ Add study to spawn a new study node in the investigation graph (seeds a child study.yaml from the proposal, with a leads-to edge back to this study).

Engineered vs autopoietic reproduction (negative control)
Why: Show the metric must couple reproduction to self-maintenance — copying without metabolism should not qualify (review C7).

Conclusion synthesis

Read-only synthesis derived from the study's canonical fields (findings, limitations, follow-up proposals).

Claims
  • The precarious identity is inherited: one founder grows and divides into a population of 300, and noisy partitioning + heritable mutation diversify the lineages (trait spread 0.11). One individual becomes a population of non-identical individuals.
  • Reproduction is precarious: 10% of daughters inherit too little membrane to maintain themselves and dissolve. Division has a viability cost — the same self-maintenance, now across generations.
Evidence
  • 0.1142
  • 0.0956
Next steps
  • Engineered vs autopoietic reproduction (negative control)

Pipeline gate & conclusion logic (technical)

Prerequisites: study-3-adaptive-chemotaxis (tests-passed)

Enables: study-5-selection (tests-passed)

Proceed when: A founder reproduces into a population whose individuals differ in composition, and division carries a viability cost (some daughters dissolve).

Theoretical commitment

What this study adds to its prerequisite — the component introduced, the deficit it closes, the new behavior it unlocks, the earlier invariants it must preserve, and the alternatives it excludes.

Invariant checks

Earlier guarantees re-checked in the current code state — prior vs current value and whether each was preserved. Invalidated / weakened first.

StudyTestPriorNowStatus
study-1-membrane-metabolism-loopoperational-closure00preserved
study-1-membrane-metabolism-loopprecariousness0.03510.035136preserved

Biology — what this study is about

The framework REJECTS an externally-maintained mimic: clamped from outside it persists when starved (precariousness fails), so the metric does not mistake external upkeep for self-production.

The framework REJECTS a network missing self-production: removing the membrane producer leaves a non-empty closure gap (['membrane_lipids']), so a structurally-incomplete network does not pass operational closure.

Overview

Turn the demonstration into a test: challenge the autopoiesis framework with systems that should NOT qualify, and check that the metric REJECTS them. We recorded 2 findings confirm the expected biology. Gate decision: In progress. Continue analysing run outcomes.

Discovery implications

Where this study's results leave the mechanism model — and what to investigate next.

✓ Resolved uncertainties

  • The precariousness and closure criteria reject externally-maintained and structurally-incomplete systems — they have discriminative power, not just confirmatory fit.

● Remaining uncertainties

  • Only two adversarial archetypes are probed; parasitic / partially-dependent and random-cyclic networks remain to be tested.

Alternate hypotheses (1)

A cleverly-tuned random cyclic network might still score a closed gap.
closure is a structural bookkeeping check; a hand-crafted cycle could satisfy it without biological meaning.
Discriminating observables: operational_closure gap on a randomly-wired network, precariousness of a parasitic agent that exploits a host

Follow-up study proposals (2)

Click ➕ Add study to spawn a new study node in the investigation graph (seeds a child study.yaml from the proposal, with a leads-to edge back to this study).

Parasitic / partially-dependent system
Why: Blur self-production vs dependence (parasites, endosymbionts, reduced genomes) and check where the criteria draw the line.
Random cyclic network
Why: Test whether a meaningless cyclic network can be made to score a closed gap (would expose closure as mere bookkeeping).

How do we judge success? (2 tests — 2 ⏳ pending)

Each test makes a specific scientific claim with a machine-checkable criterion (measure + pass_if). Tests are now evaluated by code against the run (the run/outcome spine: RunReader → evaluator): the pill shows the result, and the evidence line shows the measured value, whether it was computed by code or routed to an agent, and whether the code verdict agrees with the authored one (reconcile). ⏳ pending = the study hasn't run yet. Technical assertion + the exact evaluator are under "Technical details".

  • The framework REJECTS a network missing self-production: removing the membrane producer leaves a non-empty closure gap (['membrane_lipids']), so a structurally-incomplete network does not pass operational closure.
